listening.receptive: Recognize familiar words and basic phrases.
listening.interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.

reading.reading1: Find familiar words in a text.
reading.reading2: Identify one-step written directions with visual support.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.productive: Use basic words or phrases to identify time words.
speaking.interactive: Exchange simple questions and answers about the current day.

writing.productive: Write their own name and other very familiar words without assistance.
writing.interactive: Draw pictures related to a simple sentence they have written.

mediation.text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.

vocabulary: Nouns: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, weekend, day, week
Verbs: be (am, is, are)
Adverbs of time: today, tomorrow
Numbers: 1-20
Adjectives: relaxing, exciting, busy, quiet
Prepositions of time: on, before, after
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Recognizing sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar words related to days of the week and routines

pragmatic: Exchanging questions and answers: Exchanging questions and answers about the current day using simple phrases like "Today is Monday."
Using time-related vocabulary: Identifying words for days and weekends by using or pointing to them
sociolinguistic: Classroom schedules: Using polite expressions when talking about the days of the week and routines.

listening.receptive: Follow very simple directions and instructions.
listening.interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.

reading.reading1: Identify one-step written directions with visual support.
reading.reading2: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.productive: Use basic words or phrases to identify time words.
speaking.interactive: Exchange simple questions and answers about the current day.

writing.productive: Fill in a form with their name and age with adult help.
writing.interactive: Write simple phrases with errors on a drawing or postcard.

mediation.text: Draw a single picture that represents a simple story or event.
mediation.communication: Use gestures to show how to play a very simple game.

vocabulary: Nouns: numbers 1-20, weather, sun, rain, clouds, wind, day, storm, snow
Verbs: rain, shine, blow, fall, clear up
Adjectives: sunny, rainy, cloudy, warm, cold, cool, clear, windy
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Initial sound of letter /w/ in the word "weather" and identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar vocabulary (e.g., /r/ in "rainy")

pragmatic: Talking about weather: Describe simple weather conditions (e.g., "It is sunny.") and describe simple reactions to weather (e.g., "I'm cold." or "I'm hot.")
Asking questions about weather: Ask basic questions, such as "What is the weather today?" to learn about daily conditions
sociolinguistic: Weather conversations: Engaging in simple weather-related conversations.

listening.receptive: Identify the topic of a conversation that involves familiar subjects.
listening.interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.

reading.reading1: Identify pictures that show what happened in a simple story heard.
reading.reading2: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.interactive: Express likes or dislikes using simple words and pointing.

writing.productive: Write numbers up to 10 with guidance.
writing.interactive: Write one-word answers to simple questions.

mediation.text: Identify a few familiar words from a story by connecting them to prior knowledge.

grammar: Present simple (e.g., "It is an orange cat.")
vocabulary: Nouns: bird, flower, cat, tree, house, leaf, sky
Verbs: see, look, fly, sit, grow, count, be (is), color, show
Adjectives: blue, purple, orange, green, red, tall, small
Determiners/Pronouns: that, this
Quantifiers: two, three, four, many
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Identifying initial sounds of letters of the alphabet in common objects and things (e.g., /f/ in "fish," /b/ in "bird," /k/ in "cat")

pragmatic: Recognizing colors and objects: Identify familiar objects by color (e.g., "blue bird" or "red flower.")
Describing with colors: Use simple color phrases in sentences (e.g., "That is a red bird.")
Counting and identifying colors of objects: Identify and count familiar objects while specifying their color (e.g., "three blue birds" or "two red flowers")
sociolinguistic: Observing surroundings: Describing objects using color and shape while considering their cultural or personal significance.

listening.receptive: Show comprehension of basic expressions for greeting and leave-taking if supported by gestures.
listening.interactive: Answer simple questions about oneself, using gestures or formulaic expressions.

reading.reading1: Recognize when written, without visual support, not only their own name but also the names of classmates or family members.
reading.reading2: Point to simple words in the text that identify objects or characters in a picture book.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.productive: Locate objects and people, using words.
speaking.interactive: Respond nonverbally to simple yes/no questions by nodding or shaking head.

writing.productive: Write their own name and other very familiar words without assistance.
writing.interactive: Draw pictures related to a simple sentence they have written.

mediation.text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.
mediation.communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.

vocabulary: Nouns: family, mom, dad, brother, sister, aunt, uncle, cousin, grandmother, grandfather, pet, home
Verbs: be (is, are), love, live, play, visit, hug, help, talk, smile
Adjectives: old, young, friendly, tall, short
Personal pronouns: she, he
Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Matching letter sounds to words, such as /b/ in "brother" and /s/ in "sister"

pragmatic: Introducing family members: Use simple sentences to describe family (e.g., "She is my mom.")
Asking about family: Respond to basic questions about family members (e.g., "Who is he?" "He is my brother.")
sociolinguistic: Describing Family Roles: Using appropriate language to describe family members and their roles (e.g., "This is my mother.").

listening.receptive: Catch the main idea of a short, simple story or announcement with visual support or illustrations.
listening.interactive: Identify objects in the immediate environment in response to simple questions.

reading.reading1: Sequence pictures to demonstrate comprehension of texts that include a clear beginning, middle, and end.
reading.reading2: Show comprehension of the main action or event in a very simple storybook with pictures.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.productive: Name common objects and people, including family members.
speaking.interactive: Take part in very simple classroom games using one or two repeated lines.

writing.productive: Fill in a form with their name and age with adult help.
writing.interactive: Write very simple sentences with assistance, even if errors are present.

mediation.text: Draw a single picture that represents a simple story or event.
mediation.communication: Lead a simple classroom activity like a lineup or cleanup using gestures and basic words.

grammar: Present simple (e.g., "It is a red bike.")
vocabulary: Nouns: car, bus, bike, truck, train, taxi
Verbs: drive, ride, see, go, stop, be (is)
Adjectives: green, blue, red, yellow, purple, blue, red, orange, small, big, old, new
Demonstratives: this, that
Preposition of place: on
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Initial sound at the beginning of vehicle-related vocabulary (e.g., /k/ in "car") and identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in familiar vocabulary (e.g., /y/ in "yellow")

pragmatic: Describing objects: When describing colors in transportation, use phrases that describe vehicles, like "It is a red bike." "That is a green car." or "It's a blue bus."
Answering questions about colors and objects: Respond with one-word answers or gestures for vehicles and colors (e.g., "Point to the blue bus.")
sociolinguistic: Norms of politeness for making requests: Using culturally appropriate norms of politeness in communication when describing transportation (e.g., "Please, point to the blue car.").

listening.receptive: Recognize simple classroom instructions spoken slowly and clearly.
listening.interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.

reading.reading1: Recognize familiar words in simple texts.
reading.reading2: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.productive: Name common classroom objects and familiar people.
speaking.interactive: Engage with very simple classroom commands.

writing.productive: Write numbers up to ten with guidance.
writing.interactive: Write one-word answers to simple questions.

mediation.communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.

vocabulary: Nouns: square, rectangle, circle, triangle, oval, diamond, star
Personal pronoun: it
Verbs: see, look, draw, find, be (is)
Adjectives: round, square, tall, long, short, big, small
Demonstratives: this, that
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Shape names, initial sounds in names of shapes (e.g., /s/ in "square")

pragmatic: Identifying shapes: Recognize and name shapes like squares and circles during classroom activities
Describing shapes: Use simple phrases to talk about shapes (e.g., "This is a rectangle.")
sociolinguistic: Shape and color recognition: Describing characteristics of objects around using the names of shapes and colors (e.g., "The sun is yellow.").

listening.receptive: Recognize familiar words and basic phrases.
listening.interactive: Identify objects in the immediate environment in response to simple questions.

reading.reading1: Recognize familiar words in simple texts.
reading.reading2: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

writing.productive: Label common items using a model to copy from.
writing.interactive: Write simple phrases with errors on a drawing or postcard.

mediation.text: Use pictures or objects to express ideas.

vocabulary: Nouns: pineapple, lemon, lime, mango, papaya, cracker, banana, food, juice box, snack, meal
Verbs: eat, cut, hold, see, mix
Adjectives: round, square, oval, big, small, healthy, tasty, orange, black, brown, white
Demonstratives: this, that
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Recognizing sounds of letters of the alphabet in names of shapes and foods (e.g., /æ/ in "apple")

pragmatic: Identifying food shapes: Identifying and describing foods by shape (e.g., "The cracker is square.").
Asking about shapes in food: Engage in simple questions about food items (e.g., "What shape is the apple?")
sociolinguistic: Describing food: Using simple words to describe the colors and shapes of fruits and other foods, such as "orange" and "cracker.".

listening.receptive: Catch the main idea of a short, simple story or announcement with visual support or illustrations.
listening.interactive: Respond to simple conversations on familiar topics by using gestures or by pointing.

reading.reading1: Match simple words to corresponding pictures.
reading.reading2: Identify simple words for very common symbols.
reading.phonemic_awareness: Identify sounds of the letters of the alphabet when supported with a concrete item or picture representing that sound.

speaking.interactive: Express likes or dislikes using simple words and pointing.

writing.interactive: Write very simple sentences with assistance, even if errors are present.

mediation.communication: Use gestures and demonstrations to help classmates perform a simple task.

grammar: Noun + verb + adjective (e.g., "The flowers are pink.")
Present simple (e.g., "This is a tall tree.")
vocabulary: Nouns: garden, tree, flower, plant, leaf, bush, seed, vine, soil, sun
Verbs: grow, seed, plant, water, bloom, sprout, be (is/are/am)
Adjectives: pink, green, tall, short, small, big, leafy, little
Question word: what
Demonstratives: this, that
Possessive pronouns: my, your, his, her
pronunciation: Phonemic awareness: Identification of sounds of letters of the alphabet in nature-related vocabulary (e.g., /s/ in "sun")

pragmatic: Talking about nature: Describe plants and trees with singular and plural phrases (e.g., "The tree is tall." "The flowers are pink.")
Asking questions in nature: Use basic questions like "What color is the flower?" to learn about plants
sociolinguistic: Respect for nature: Talking about plants and trees using respectful language (e.g., "I like plants.").
